1. Project Overview

This system aims to improve maternal and fetal outcomes by enabling early detection of High-Risk Pregnancy, IUGR, and Fetal Distress using AI, clinical scoring systems, and automated referral pathways.

The solution integrates:

Tier 1: ANM/ASHA/PHC-level screening using clinical data

Tier 2: AI-based diagnostics using USG Biometry and CTG trace analysis

Tier 3: Alerting, referral, and identifying nearest Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA) centers

2. Primary Objectives

Provide instant High-Risk Pregnancy Scoring (e.g., Coopland’s).

Detect IUGR using USG biometry extraction + ML.

Detect Fetal Distress using CTG data classification.

Enable automated alerts and referral recommendations.

Reduce referral delays by pointing users to nearest PMSMA centers.

3. System Outputs (Developer Must Deliver)
User will receive:

Risk Score → Low / Moderate / High

IUGR Risk Output → Normal Growth / Suspected FGR / Confirmed FGR

CTG Classification → Normal / Suspicious / Pathological

Referral Action → Visit PHC / FRU / District Hospital

Nearest PMSMA Centers with:

Name

Address

GPS distance

Monthly PMSMA camp date

Alerts:

SMS to Medical Officer

Dashboard red-flag alerts

4. System Architecture
4.1 High-Level Architecture
User (ANM/Doctor)
        |
        v
Front-End App (Web/Mobile)
        |
        v
Backend API Layer (Node/Python)
        |
        ├── Clinical Scoring Engine
        ├── USG OCR + IUGR Model
        ├── CTG Classifier Model
        ├── PMSMA Locator Service (GIS)
        └── SMS/Notification Service
        |
        v
Database (FHIR-Compatible)

5. Module Specifications
5.1 Module A — Clinical Data Input & High-Risk Score
Required Inputs

Age

Parity

Previous C-section

BP

Hb

Diabetes

Multiple Pregnancy

Bleeding

Edema

Fundal Height vs GA mismatch

Any other risk factor

Output

Coopland Score (0–37)

Category: Low / Moderate / High Risk

5.2 Module B — USG Image → IUGR Detection
Input

Uploaded USG image containing biometry

OCR Extracted Fields

BPD

HC

AC

FL

EFW (if present)

Gestational Age

ML Logic

Compare EFW percentile with WHO/INTERGROWTH-21 standards

Detect asymmetrical or symmetrical IUGR patterns

Output

Normal Growth

Suspected IUGR

Confirmed IUGR

Growth Curve

5.3 Module C — CTG CSV Data Analysis
CTG Features (Must Be Used in ML):
Feature Name	Description
baseline_value	Average FHR (110–160 normal)
accelerations	Count of FHR increases
fetal_movement	Number of detected movements
uterine_contractions	Frequency/intensity
light_decelerations	Mild FHR drops
severe_decelerations	Dangerous FHR drops
prolonged_decelerations	>2 minutes drop
abnormal_short_term_variability	Reduced beat-to-beat variability
mean_value_of_short_term_variability	Avg STV
percentage_of_time_with_abnormal_long_term_variability	% abnormal LTV
mean_value_of_long_term_variability	Avg LTV
histogram_width	Spread of FHR
histogram_min	Min value
histogram_max	Max value
histogram_number_of_peaks	Histogram peaks
histogram_number_of_zeroes	Empty bins
histogram_mode	Most frequent value
histogram_mean	Average FHR
histogram_median	Median FHR
histogram_variance	Variance
histogram_tendency	Up/down trend
Output

Normal

Suspicious

Pathological

5.4 Module D – PMSMA Center Locator
Requirements:

Use Government PMSMA Open Data (static or API).

Implement GPS distance calculation.

Display:

Center name

Address

Monthly PMSMA day (usually 9th of every month)

Contact number

5.5 Module E — Alerts & Referral
When High Risk →

Send SMS + Dashboard notification:

Mother’s Name

Risk type

Recommended facility

GPS link

6. System Workflow (User Journey)

Login → Role-based (ANM / Doctor / Admin)

Enter clinical data → Get Risk Score

Upload USG image → OCR → IUGR result

Upload CTG CSV/image → ML classification

System maps nearest PMSMA centre

System recommends referral action

Alerts sent to Medical Officer & PHC

Case added to dashboard for monitoring

7. Tech Stack Requirements
Frontend

Flutter / React

Offline support required at PHC level

Backend

Node.js or Python FastAPI

REST + JSON

JWT Authentication

AI Models

TensorFlow/PyTorch

ONNX Runtime for mobile inference

Database

PostgreSQL

FHIR data structure compliant

Hosting

AWS / Azure / GCP

Separate ML inference server recommended

8. API Requirements
1. POST /risk-score

Input: clinical fields
Output: risk category + score

2. POST /usg-analyze

Input: image
Output: extracted values + IUGR prediction

3. POST /ctg-analyze

Input: CTG CSV
Output: Normal/Suspicious/Pathological

4. GET /pmsma-centers?lat=xx&lon=yy

Output: nearest centers

5. POST /alert

Send SMS/notification

9. Testing Requirements

Validate OCR accuracy ≥ 92%

CTG classification accuracy ≥ 95%

App offline support testing

API stress test for PHC use

10. Expected Impact (With Statistics)

Reduce referral delays by 40–60%

Reduce preeclampsia ICU admissions by 30%

Reduce CTG misinterpretation errors by 50%

Improve early IUGR detection from 26% → 70%

Enable timely maternal care access to 200M+ rural women (India)

11. Deliverables

Complete mobile + web app

Admin dashboard

Integrated AI models (USG + CTG)

PMSMA locator module

API + database + documentation

Deployment + testing + maintenance

**Designing the Application**

I've been focusing on the React web application design for the Maternal Health System. I'm prioritizing the user interface, aiming for intuitive clinical data input and score calculation (like Coopland's) as well as the initial user interface for ultrasound data and fetal heart rate trending.


**Expanding Feature Set**

I've significantly broadened my focus to incorporate the crucial functionalities.  I'm now integrating Ultrasound (USG) image analysis with OCR, and Fetal Distress classification using CTG data, both leveraging the Gemini API.  I'm also working on the PMSMA center locator and visualizing risks within a central dashboard.


**Defining the Data Structures**

I'm currently structuring the data types needed for patient information, ultrasound biometry, and cardiotocography data. I'm also planning the design and implementation of the Gemini service methods to simulate OCR and ML tasks.


**Analyzing System Output**

My focus has shifted to coding the system's simulated outputs. I'm building a frontend that leverages Gemini for all analysis. I'm starting by establishing data types in `types.ts` and implementing Gemini API calls using `gemini-2.5-flash` with structured JSON responses via `responseSchema`. I'm also including mock location service for the PMSMA locator functionality.  I'll use `metadata.json` to organize the app.


**Outlining Component Structure**

I've outlined the core component structure now. I've defined `Layout.tsx` for the navigation bar, `RiskAssessment.tsx` for the clinical form, and `USGAnalysis.tsx` for image handling.  I'm also working on `CTGAnalysis.tsx` for cardiotocography data, `PMSMALocator.tsx` for the map view, and `Dashboard.tsx` for the overview.  I'm thinking about routing strategy within `App.tsx` and have `index.tsx` for entry and `index.html` for the HTML structure.


**Refining Risk Analysis**

I'm now prioritizing the risk assessment logic. I've decided to employ a state-based navigation for simplicity, contrasting the earlier routing-based approach. To ensure UI stability, I'm integrating the `geminiService` with a strong reliance on `responseSchema`. For the Coopland score, I'll leverage Gemini to generate a nuanced AI opinion, including specific referral suggestions, as it aligns better with the required AI component and back-end architecture. The USG Logic is currently in progress: user uploads image -> Convert to Base64 ->


**Developing Core Features**

I'm now diving into the code.  I'm focusing on the Ultrasound (USG) image analysis with Gemini, defining a prompt to extract biometry and estimate EFW, with IUGR assessment, and implementing the same for the CTG CSV file parsing. I'm hardcoding PMSMA centers for geolocation testing, planning to build the logic around user-provided CSV inputs, parse, and obtain JSON outputs from the Gemini API regarding CTG classifications. It's time to build the code!
